title: Inexorable
content:
My thoughts hold mortal strife;
I do detest my life,
And with lamenting cries
Peace to my soul to bring
Oft call that prince which here doth monarchise:
But he, grim-grinning King,
Who caitiffs scorns, and doth the blest surprise,
Late having deck�d with beauty�s rose his tomb,
Disdains to crop a weed, and will not come.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Henry Drummond delivers a powerful performance in "Inexorable"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
